executive_summary.research_scope: 1000 verbatim quotes from 78 sources across 10+ platforms
executive_summary.key_finding: The slice-suffering golfer market is massive, emotionally charged, and underserved by solutions that work without sacrificing distance
executive_summary.primary_opportunity: Position SF2 as the ONLY driver that fixes your slice WITHOUT sacrificing distance - a claim no premium OEM makes
executive_summary.market_size_indicator: Slice mentioned in 312 of 1000 quotes (31%) - most frequent single term after 'driver'

top_5_validated_opportunities.insight: Every draw bias driver on market requires distance sacrifice. Users accept '10-15 yard loss' as unavoidable. If SF2 delivers slice correction WITH distance, it's category-creating.
top_5_validated_opportunities.action: Lead with dual promise: 'Fix your slice AND gain distance'

top_5_validated_opportunities.insight: Driver anxiety is a psychological condition as much as a technical one. Users describe 'terror', 'dread', and 'mental defeat'. The emotional benefit of confidence may outweigh the technical benefit.
top_5_validated_opportunities.action: Include confidence/mental game messaging in all campaigns

top_5_validated_opportunities.insight: Significant frustration with lessons/tips that promise but don't deliver. SF2 can position as 'the solution when nothing else worked'.
Large, affluent segment watching distance disappear year over year. Willing to invest in solutions. Strong nostalgia angle.
Golf is social. Being outdriven, holding up groups, and 'making a fool of yourself' creates powerful purchase motivation.
top_5_validated_opportunities.action: Include 'keep up with your buddies' in messaging

pain_headlines: I could probably send the ball 2 fairways to the right
I was always playing military golf
Distance was gone, I mean G-O-N-E gone
I make a fool of myself with 460 CC head
Nothing has worked and now I'm just frustrated
transformation_headlines: I never thought I'd lose my slice but it's gone
85% of the time now I'm straight
Best decision I've made in a long time
credibility_builders: I will never buy an off the shelf driver again
Changed my ability to drive it in play 95% of time
Driving has been the weak link in my game - it's now a strength

competitive_white_space.current_landscape: All draw bias drivers position as 'accuracy' solutions with implicit distance sacrifice
competitive_white_space.sf2_opportunity: First to credibly claim BOTH slice-fix AND distance - not either/or
competitor_vulnerabilities: Premium OEMs: Complex technology, high price, still require fitting

objection_1.objection: Equipment can't fix a bad swing
objection_1.response_strategy: Acknowledge swing matters, but show how SF2 works WITH your swing - not against it. Use fitting transformation testimonials as proof.

objection_2.response_strategy: Reframe: It's not masking the problem, it's giving your swing what it's been missing. Compare to properly fitted clubs.

objection_3.response_strategy: Use 'tried lessons, nothing worked' testimonials. Position SF2 as the solution AFTER lessons failed.

phase_1_awareness.message: Still slicing? Still losing distance? There's a reason.

phase_2_education.message: Why everything you've tried hasn't worked - and what finally will
phase_2_education.goal: Build trust, differentiate from failed solutions

phase_3_proof.message: See the results: 85% straight, 15+ yards gained

phase_4_offer.message: Risk-free trial - if it doesn't fix your slice, send it back
